data.title: Silver Plunges Record 36% as Precious Metals Suffer Historic Collapse – Bitcoin About to Rally?
data.description:
Precious metals suffered a catastrophic collapse on January 30 as gold plunged over 12% below $5,000 an ounce while silver recorded its largest intraday drop in history, falling as much as 36%, according to . Source: Bloomberg

The selloff was triggered by President Donald Trump’s nomination of Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair, which sent the dollar soaring and sparked massive profit-taking across commodities markets.

The crash wiped out more than $15 trillion from the gold and silver markets in 24 hours, an amount equal to half the size of the entire U.S. economy.

Despite the brutal correction, both metals still finished January with gains (gold up 12% and silver up 16%), while Bitcoin tumbled to a nine-month low of $82,000, raising questions about whether the digital asset will follow precious metals’ trajectory or chart its own path.Historic Selloff Driven by Warsh Nomination and Technical Factors

Spot gold prices crashed more than 12% at one point, hitting a low of $4,682 per ounce in its biggest single-day decline since the early 1980s, closing down 9.25% at $4,880.

Silver experienced an even more dramatic collapse, plummeting 36% intraday to $74.28 per ounce before settling 26.42% lower at $85.259, marking its worst day since March 1980.

Gold and silver have lost a combined $6.52 trillion over the last 48 hours.That is equivalent to Bitcoin's entire market cap nearly 4 times over.Wild. — Joe Consorti (@JoeConsorti)

“Trump announcing Warsh as his pick for next Fed Chair has been a US dollar positive and precious metals negative,” Aakash Doshi, global head of gold and metals strategy at State Street Investment Management, told Bloomberg.

“This has probably been exacerbated by month-end rebalancing as both short dollar and long precious metals has been the consensus macro trade over the past two to three weeks.“

The selloff accelerated through forced selling and margin calls as leveraged positions unwound.

“This is getting crazy,” Matt Maley, equity strategist at Miller Tabak, adding, “Most of this is probably ‘forced selling.’ This has been the hottest asset for day traders and other short-term traders recently. So, there has been some leverage built up in silver. With the huge decline today, the margin calls went out.“

Bloomberg also noted that technical factors amplified the crash as a gamma squeeze forced dealers to sell futures contracts as prices fell through key options levels at $5,300, $5,200, and $5,100 for gold.

Gold’s relative-strength index had recently hit 90, the highest in decades, signaling that the precious metal was severely overbought and due for a correction.Source: Bloomberg

Major mining companies suffered devastating losses, with Newmont down 11.52%, Barrick Gold falling 12.09%, and AngloGold plunging 13.28%.

Copper also retreated 3.4% from Thursday’s record high above $14,000 per ton, while silver ETFs saw their worst days on record, with the iShares Silver Trust losing 31%.Bitcoin Faces ‘Two-Path’ Dilemma as Markets Reassess Fed Policy

Bitcoin dropped to $82,000 following Warsh’s nomination, with spot Bitcoin ETF outflows accelerating to roughly $1 billion this month and total liquidations approaching $800 million to $1 billion, according to Bitfinex analysts.

The digital asset is now trading at a nine-month low as investors reassess monetary policy trajectories.Source: TradingView

Jeff Park, CIO at Bitwise, for understanding Bitcoin’s divergent path from precious metals in his “Two Bitcoin Thesis.”

“Metals are telling you spot debasement is happening; Bitcoin will tell you when the yield curve itself breaks,” Park explained, distinguishing between “negative rho Bitcoin” that performs better when rates fall and “positive rho Bitcoin” that thrives when financial system assumptions collapse.

Park argued the current environment represents the worst scenario for Bitcoin’s “negative rho” thesis.

“We’re currently experiencing good deflation in technology sectors while avoiding bad deflation in credit markets,” he wrote.

“This is the worst possible environment for Bitcoin: productive enough to keep growth assets attractive, stable enough to keep Treasuries credible, but not catastrophic enough to break the system.“

— Jeff Park (@dgt10011)

Speaking with Cryptonews, Aurélie Barthere, Principal Research Analyst at Nansen, identified multiple negative catalysts driving Bitcoin lower: “Fed Chair Powell guiding for no Fed cut in its remaining mandate till June 2026, President Trump seemingly choosing the more hawkish candidate as the new Fed Chair, Kevin Warsh, and a BTC correlation with US equities turning positive again.“

Flow data shows “slow capitulation in ETFs, options, and miner activity,” she noted.

Eric Jackson, incoming CEO of EMJX-SRX Health, offered a contrarian view on Warsh’s nomination.

“The nomination of Kevin Warsh for Federal Reserve Chairman appears constructively neutral-to-positive for crypto over the medium term, even if the initial market reaction is cautious,” Jackson told Cryptonews.

“His emphasis on balance sheet discipline and clearer boundaries between Treasury and the Fed points to less reflexive quantitative easing and greater transparency around liquidity conditions.“

those who still dont understand why warsh won simply dont understand the unspoken truth that the fed-treasury relationship is the most powerful lever to affect the kind of generational change that this system now requiresit was always warsh and bessentincredibly optimistic— Jeff Park (@dgt10011)

Park’s analysis suggests the Warsh appointment could ultimately prove bullish for Bitcoin’s “positive rho” scenario by accelerating a systemic reckoning.

“If you believe the debt trajectory is unsustainable, if you believe fiscal dominance will eventually override monetary orthodoxy, if you believe the risk-free rate will eventually be revealed as a fiction, then you want Warsh,” he wrote.

He concluded that while he cannot confirm if “$82k was indeed the bottom,” historically, “bottoms are almost always noted by a radical shift in market regime that fundamentally resets investor behavior.“

data.title: Buy Microsoft's stock while it's down? First ask yourself this question.
data.description:
By Emily Bary

Microsoft's stock is potentially compelling for long-term investors who believe the business is fundamentally 'better than average,' portfolio manager says

Microsoft didn't give investors enough to be excited about with its most recent earnings report.

Microsoft's stock hasn't been this cheap in almost three years. But what, exactly, investors should do about that is increasingly a topic of Wall Street debate.

Sentiment toward Microsoft's stock (MSFT) was already bleak heading into its latest earnings report, and it only got worse despite what Matt Stucky, chief portfolio manager for equities at Northwestern Mutual Wealth Management, called "reasonably good" numbers. Microsoft shares slid 10% on Thursday to log their worst day in more than 12 years.

The bearish view concerns whether Microsoft can bring its customer base into the world of AI while fending off competition from upstarts like Anthropic, Stucky said.

But bulls, he noted, might glimpse a similar setup to what Alphabet's stock (GOOG) (GOOGL) faced last spring: Investors were deeply pessimistic about the company's AI positioning before Gemini advancements helped swiftly change the mood.

More from MarketWatch: Google rose to the top in the AI race. Here's what it'll take to stay there.

Investors wondering whether to buy the dip might be well served by asking themselves a simple question, Stucky suggested: "Do you believe that Microsoft is a better-than-average company? Because it's priced as an average company." (Trading at a nearly three-year-low multiple of 23.9-times forward earnings, Microsoft's stock is a bit more expensive than the S&P 500 SPX, trading at 22.3-times forward earnings, according to FactSet data.)

The knock on Microsoft is that its Azure cloud-computing business isn't growing fast enough to justify the company's AI spending. Azure grew 38% in the latest quarter on a constant-currency basis, below bullish buy-side hopes. But Stucky said the business could be growing at something like a 50% clip if Microsoft shifted more of its limited compute resources toward the cloud and away from internal developers and first-party applications.

See also: Microsoft's stock may be 'dead money' even after historic $357 billion market-cap wipeout

It may be smart that the company is making that short-term sacrifice, however, since Stucky said Microsoft's first-party software applications are a bigger financial opportunity in the long run.

"The client diversification is higher, the margins are higher and the long-term value" is higher, he noted. Because these are services that will be sold to existing customers, "there's no customer-acquisition cost, so it's like straight margin once they have have this stuff built."

For a long-term investor, Stucky thinks Microsoft is "an interesting name to look at." He's just not sure what would be the specific catalyst that gets the stock going again.

Evercore ISI analyst Kirk Materne agreed that investing in Microsoft's stock is a game of patience at this point.

"Our takeaway from Microsoft's earnings call was not that the fundamental picture is materially different today versus yesterday, it's that the company left investors with no near-term catalyst" - whereas other "Magnificent Seven" companies, like Meta Platforms (META), "are accelerating," he wrote Thursday evening.

The same advice applies to the software sector in general. "Until the volatility subsides a bit, the rally in [semiconductor stocks] flattens out or we get some better optics in the space ... software investors are going to need to take a long-term view," Materne added in a subsequent note to clients.

Read: Software stocks suffer steep selloff, widening the gap with hot chip stocks

-Emily Bary

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: AI-Era Position Statement to Protect the Integrity of Healthcare, Technology, and Services Benchmarking published by Black Book Research
data.description:
Black Book outlines an AI-era integrity architecture for healthcare benchmarking, instrumentation hardening, tiered verification, real-time anomaly detection, and longitudinal observability, paired with transparent, audit-ready reporting and responsible AI use with clear human accountability.

TAMPA, FL / ACCESS Newswire / January 31, 2026 / Black Book Research today announced the publication of its position statement, "Market Research Integrity and Insight in the AI Era," establishing a comprehensive framework to safeguard survey, polling, and satisfaction-based research against emerging risks accelerated by generative AI while using AI responsibly to improve research operations without compromising the truth source of benchmark findings.

Generative AI is simultaneously increasing the speed of legitimate research workflows and lowering the cost of synthetic participation, scripted completion, and scaled fraud. These integrity risks can distort vendor performance evaluations, technology selection decisions, managed services assessments, and market perception signals across healthcare, enterprise technology, services, marketing support/PR, and the broader market research and satisfaction measurement industries.

"Health systems are under pressure to make faster, higher-confidence decisions about technology, services, and operations," said Doug Brown, Founder of Black Book Research. "AI helps us improve measurement quality, accelerate insight delivery, and strengthen transparency but our benchmarks remain grounded in verified human experience, governed methods, and audit-ready documentation so executives can act with confidence in decisions that ultimately affect patient care and experience."

Black Book's position statement formalizes a defense framework built on three pillars:

1) Policy

Human-response standard: Benchmark studies are designed to measure real human experience, judgment, and role-based workflow reality; AI-generated respondent answers are not treated as valid participation unless a study explicitly defines a synthetic-data use case.

Responsible internal AI use: AI may assist operational efficiency (e.g., instrument QA support, translation support, anomaly detection), while methodological decisions and reporting remain human-led and accountable.

Fit-for-purpose assurance: Integrity controls scale with decision risk.

2) Safeguards

Defense-in-depth controls spanning instrument design, recruitment, fielding, validation, and incentive governance.

Bot-ballot and automation resistance designed into surveys through dynamic routing, randomized elements, comprehension-dependent items, and real-time anomaly gating.

Tiered respondent verification for healthcare IT (HIT) research to reduce synthetic eligibility. especially in studies where role authenticity and workflow exposure determine validity.

3) Transparency

Standardized documentation of assurance posture, inclusion/exclusion logic, and fit-for-purpose interpretation guidance.

A Data Integrity Summary available for major deliverables to support procurement, governance review, and audit-readiness.

Longitudinal integrity observability and pre-AI baselining

Black Book's integrity approach is strengthened by its governed longitudinal research database, including more than 4 million historical data points collected PRIOR to the broad availability of generative AI to respondents. This pre-AI baseline supports comparability across waves, drift detection, and quality observability over time, including integrity signals and outcomes monitored through Black Book's analytics environment and Google Looker evidence layer.

AI as a force multiplier with non-negotiable guardrails

Within a clear boundary that benchmark findings represent verified human experience, Black Book applies AI to improve instrument quality, fielding predictability, integrity monitoring, and decision-ready reporting without changing underlying results, substituting automated judgment for methodological decisions, or allowing commercial influence to shape outcomes.

Availability

The full position statement, "Market Research Integrity and Insight in the AI Era," is available from Black Book Research and can be read on LinkedIn at https://www.linkedin.com/posts/blackbookmarketresearchllc_ai-is-changing-how-research-gets-done-and-activity-7423193929635909632-1CQS?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAACTtTEBqAlZREcfAJvhEf9l7mUHd9EfpNY and Medium.com at https://medium.com/@research_22835/healthcare-market-research-integrity-and-insight-in-the-ai-era-be53e7fea2c1 or at https://www.blackbookmarketresearch.com

About Black Book Research

Black Book Research is an independent, healthcare-centric market research and public opinion research firm providing global competitive intelligence, satisfaction measurement, and benchmarking across healthcare IT, technology, and services. Black Book's research is designed to reflect real-world user experience, supported by governed methods, transparent reporting, and independence from vendor influence. Media contact research@blackbookmarketresearch.com

SOURCE: Black Book Research

data.title: 7 Reasons Teens Say No to AI — WSJ
data.description:
By Julie Jargon

While adults worry about sharpening their artificial-intelligence skills to remain relevant in their jobs, many of their teenage children are feeling meh about the AI revolution.

I wondered if it was just my teenagers, whose eyes glaze over every time my husband and I urge them to learn more about AI. Parents can't help but worry about how a lack of AI preparedness will affect their kids' future career prospects.

I reached out to dozens of teens and found many who share my kids' reluctance to embrace AI — as well as their fears about where it's headed.

"I think it's bad for creativity," said Willow Nolan, a 16-year-old in Los Angeles. "You just put the prompt in and it makes whatever you want. When you use it excessively, it's hard to have ideas of your own."

It isn't that teens don't use chatbots — about two-thirds do, according to a recent report from Pew Research Center. But the teens I spoke to say they have curtailed their use because of fears over cheating accusations, concerns about AI's environmental impact and worries about negative psychological effects.

"I will use AI sometimes, but it's a last resort for me," said Leo Gurney, a 19-year-old college student in London.

Whether this represents a small group of holdouts or a coming generational shift remains to be seen. We know generative AI is capable of bringing positive changes in the form of scientific discovery, office-productivity improvements, support for people who are lonely and much more.

Yet these teens, who have grown up witnessing the problems wrought by social media, are taking a cautious approach. These are their biggest concerns.

AI can diminish human relationships

"It freaks me out that people are choosing to talk to fake people instead of socializing, and I worry it could become an addiction," said Annika Frederick, a 14-year-old in Denver who only occasionally uses ChatGPT — and always in a web browser. "I don't want to commit to downloading it on my phone."

Many teens (and adults) turn to AI for advice or companionship. A recent Common Sense Media survey found that about one in three teens have used chatbots for friendship, romantic interactions or emotional support.

"We don't know the lasting effects of talking to AI. It might make it hard to talk to people," Willow said.

AI can devalue art

"Putting in a prompt and saying 'Draw me this' doesn't feel right to me, " said Leo, who is studying esports management. "It takes the joy out of listening to a song if I learn it was made by AI, no matter how good it sounds."

"Chatbots are stealing artists' work and I have a moral objection to that," Annika said.

AI can threaten the environment

"It requires so much space to make all those data centers that house all the data that is used by AI," said Ruby Mehta, a 12-year-old in suburban Washington, D.C. "It is taking up a lot of resources, which is contributing to climate change and habitat loss and a bunch of other things."

Ohannes Montgomery, a 15-year-old in Simi Valley, Calif., said he once used ChatGPT every couple of days until three months ago, when he quit, in part, after learning how much water is used to cool the data centers.

AI can lead to laziness

"I feel like it will make people lazy. If they're stuck on something they'll always go to AI," said Chase Morgan, a 13-year-old in Grapevine, Texas.

He said when he needs tips on fixing his dirt bike, he prefers to search for things on the internet or to use YouTube. His twin brother, Pierce, however, uses AI daily. He said understanding it is a prerequisite for success.

"If you use AI for everything, you could start being dependent on it," Ohannes said.

AI can give unreliable results

Hadar Groner, an 18-year-old high-school senior in Yonkers, N.Y., said she used AI sparingly. "When I took chemistry, I would ask AI for help balancing equations and it couldn't do that. It was giving me things that didn't make sense. That was a couple of years ago, so it might have gotten better, but I always try googling things first before turning to AI."

Ruby has also gotten wrong answers, including one related to her pet snake. "I asked AI, 'What do you get a snake for their birthday?' and it said a trip to the zoo."

AI can pose privacy concerns

"I'm afraid of it storing data about me," Hadar said. "I guess that happens with any online thing, but something about sharing personal data with AI feels sketchy. I don't fully understand what they're doing with that info."

AI can be misused

Leo said he was troubled by how AI has been used to create fake nude images of people and how it has fed peoples' delusions. He read a recent story I wrote about a murder-suicide that was preceded by the killer's heavy ChatGPT use. "It gave me the shivers," he said.

"If we rely on AI too much," Leo added, "we may lose our humanity."

Write to Julie Jargon at Julie.Jargon@wsj.com

data.title: TD Sequential Flashes Buy Signal For XRP On Key Price Condition – Analyst
data.description:
The XRP price began the week with a show of bullish momentum, seeing an approximate jump of 7%. However, the altcoin could not continue on this trajectory, as it nosedived on Wednesday and continues on that downward trajectory. While XRP’s future appears to tilt towards the bearish side, a technical indicator has recently revealed that the token may be setting up for a short-term rebound.

TD Sequential Signals Potential Trend Exhaustion 

In an X post on January 30, technical analyst Ali Martinez postulates that the XRP price could soon see a rebound, provided that certain conditions are met. The central indicator for this revelation is the TD Sequential, a technical analytics tool that is used in identifying whether an uptrend or a downtrend is likely to pause, or even reverse. Simply put, the indicator tracks points of trend exhaustion, although in the short-term.

The TD Sequential has two phases, i.e., the Setup Phase (1–9 count), and the Countdown phase (up to count 13), which have their respective interpretations. When a “9” count is completed, it is typically a sign of dwindling selling pressure. On the other hand, a full “13” count is a telltale sign of an imminent reversal.

From the chart shared by the analyst, we see a completed “9” count to the downside. From this, it is apparent that the momentum driving XRP’s recent fall is nearing a point of exhaustion. Interestingly, this signal’s appearance coincides with the imminence of a key price support. Martinez explains that if the $1.70 support’s integrity is maintained, XRP stands a chance at seeing a price rebound. 

In the case where the $1.70 support sponsors a price rebound, the $1.80–$1.85 range stands as the resistance level that may test XRP’s momentum. If momentum builds and price overcomes the aforementioned price barrier, $1.90 could be another battleground.

XRP ETFs Record $69M In Net Outflows Following Thursday Purge

According to data from ETF tracking site SoSoValue, US XRP Spot ETFs are currently running at a cumulative outflow of more than $69 million. The first three days of the week produced combined positive netflows valued at 23.87 million. However, a cumulative outflow of $92.92 million on Thursday quickly flipped a positive week red. Interestingly, this negative figure is in congruence with last week’s net outflow of $40.64 million.

Typically, a negative netflow signals that institutional demand might be tapering, as it directly reports that more capital was withdrawn from the XRP ETFs than deposited. In this context, where the XRP price took on a sharp downtrend, it becomes apparent that institutional investors may have played a significant role in the price downturn of the Ripple token recently seen.

Nonetheless, negative ETF netflows do not necessarily tell a broader story of bearishness, but are reflections of profit-taking or de-risking events. As of this writing, XRP trades at a $1.74. According to data from CoinMarketCap, the altcoin has lost about 3.26% of its value since the past day.

data.title: Affle 3i Q3 profit rises 19%, as CPCU business drives growth
data.description:
Digital advertising and mobile marketing company Affle 3i Ltd reported a strong performance for the December quarter, with net profit rising 19% year-on-year to ₹119.3 crore, supported by healthy growth across industry verticals and geographies.

Revenue increased 19.2% to ₹717.4 crore from ₹601.6 crore in the year-ago period, crossing the ₹700 crore mark for the first time.

EBITDA grew 24.2% year-on-year to ₹163 crore, while operating margin improved to 22.7% from 21.8% a year earlier.

The company said its Cost Per Converted User (CPCU) business remained the key growth driver, with CPCU revenue rising 19.6% year-on-year to ₹713.6 crore in Q3 FY26. Total converted users delivered during the first nine months of FY26 stood at 33.6 crore.

Commenting on the results, Chairperson, MD & CEO Anuj Khanna Sohum said Q3 FY26 marked an important milestone with the company delivering its highest-ever quarterly revenue run-rate, EBITDA, profit and consumer conversions. He added that consistent execution and the scalability of Affle’s AI-powered Consumer Platform Stack continue to underpin performance even in a challenging global environment.

Affle said its strong balance sheet and operating cash flows will support continued investments in technology, talent and strategic initiatives under its Affle 3i growth vision. The company is also extending the capabilities of its agentic AI engine, Niko, across its consumer platform to drive higher automation and operational efficiency, while remaining focused on disciplined capital allocation and sustained innovation.

Shares of Affle 3i Ltd ended higher on Friday, January 30, by 1.58% at ₹1,559.00 on the NSE.

data.title: America's Reputation Is Faltering — WSJ
data.description:
By Cristina Lourosa-Ricardo

This is an edition of the WSJ Weekend Reads newsletter, a weekly look at our most colorful, thought-provoking and original feature stories on the business of life. If you're not subscribed, sign up here.

America First is starting to look more like America Alone.

U.S. allies in Europe and elsewhere are becoming increasingly disenchanted as President Trump continues his insults and threats.

This week we look at how that's playing out and how some allies are now seeking out alternatives to America when it comes to trade and other things. It all has long-term repercussions for everyone.

Other topics we delve into include toxic mom groups, bigger tax refunds and what happens when you stop taking Ozempic. And don't forget to test your cultural IQ.

Enjoy your weekend with these and all the great reads below.

Leading Off

America's Strained Friendships

The country's reputation abroad is taking some hits amid the foreign policy under Trump.

A Simpler Time?

Younger Americans are feeling nostalgic for the days before all the endless screen time, algorithms and AI. But not as far back as the '80s and '90s.

See the Story

A Better Winter Getaway

St. Martin is a chiller, less-crowded alternative to St. Barts, with a little French flair included.

Health

Life After Ozempic

GLP-1s, designed to be lifelong treatments for chronic diseases, are increasingly being marketed as lifestyle fixes for weight gain. So what happens when you lose the weight and stop taking them?

The Mom Cliques

Do You Know Your Culture?

Beyond the Newsroom

About Us

WSJ Weekend Reads is a showcase for the wide range of great feature writing at WSJ. Let us know what you think by emailing editor Cristina Lourosa-Ricardo at cristina.lourosa@wsj.com (if you're reading this in your inbox, you can just hit reply).

data.title: Vinati Organics Q3 profit dips 12% as revenue slides amid softer market
data.description:
Specialty chemicals manufacturer Vinati Organics reported a modest year-on-year decline in earnings for the December quarter, with net profit falling 12.2% to ₹101 crore compared with ₹115 crore in the same period last year.

Revenue slipped 3.5% to ₹531 crore from ₹550 crore, reflecting a softer operating environment.

EBITDA declined 5.2% year-on-year to ₹157.5 crore versus ₹166 crore in Q3 last year, while operating margin eased slightly to 29.7% from 30.2%.

Last year, the company had said it expects output to rise by 15% in FY26, though revenue growth is likely to be slightly lower at 10–12% due to softer product prices. Managing Director Vinati Saraf Mutreja noted that the uplift will come from capacity additions, the new acrylamide tertiary butyl sulfonic acid (ATBS) line, and recently introduced products. She added that the company is keeping its margin outlook at 27% to remain conservative while expanding into new markets.

Shares of Vinati Organics Ltd ended higher on Friday, January 30, by 1.80% at ₹1,523.50 on the NSE.

data.title: Chevron vs. Totalenergies: which is the best stock for dividends?
data.description:
Chevron Corporation, headquartered in Houston (Texas), is an American firm that traces its roots back to the renowned Standard Oil Company, founded by John D. Rockefeller in 1870. The Company is listed on the NYSE (CYX).

In Q4/2025, it recorded daily production of 4,01 million barrels, and it reported adjusted earnings of $3 billion ($1.52 per share, ahead of an LSEG consensus estimate of $1.45 per share).

Chevron focuses its business mainly in the United States (no other country accounted for 10% or more of the Company's total sales and other operating revenues), where more of its workforce is also employed (Chevron reported a total of 45,298 employees at the end of 2024).

Totalenergies, headquartered in Paris, is a French firm that was founded in 1924 on the initiative of the French Prime Minister Raymond Poincare to develop a national oil policy. It was a private company named Compagnie Francaise des Petroles (CFP).

Totalenergies is one of the largest oil companies in the World (it recorded 2.5 million barrels of oil equivalent per day in Q3/2025) with a presence in more than 130 countries. The Regions detailed below are the long-standing cornerstones of TotalEnergies:

  • Europe (the Company's decision-making centre).
  • The Middle East (where the Company has established many partnerships with producing countries and national companies).
  • Africa (where TotalEnergies has substantial oil and gas production activities and Company-branded service stations).

TotalEnergies had 102,887 employees at the end of 2024.

The Company is listed in Paris and on the NYSE.

Chevron and Totalenergies are two companies specialized in the production and sale of oil products. Thanks to their leading positions in this mature business, they used to generate solid cash flows from operating activities, which they used to pay dividends, repurchase stock, and invest in long-term projects.

The two stocks are usually inside the portfolio of income investors for the dividend returns they offer to shareholders (for example, Arnold Van Den Berg has Chevron in his portfolio while Jeremy Grantham has Totalenergies in his portfolio).

In this article, I analyze the dividends reported by them from 2012 to 2024.

I will discuss how their business strategies are expected to evolve, provide a forecast of the oil and LNG markets through 2030, and explain why I believe that Chevron is likely to generate more stable, lower-risk cash flows in the coming years than TotalEnergies, while also offering higher dividend returns.

Chevron vs. Totalenergies Dividend analysis

Period: 2012-2024

I analyzed the dividends that Chevron and Totalenergies offered to their shareholders from 2012 to 2024.

In the period of analysis, Totalenergies increased its dividend from 2.34 paid in 2012 to 3.22 paid in 2024, with the following annual increases:

  • 3.13% and 4.48% in 2018 and 2019
  • 6.05%, 6.64% and 6.52% in 2022, 2023 and 2024
  • Up to 2.46% (2013) in the other periods.

(The calculation does not consider the interim dividend of 1 paid in 2022. In 2020 (COVID), the Company paid dividends, down 1.52% from 2019).

Chevron performed better than Totalenergies because the American-based Company offered a higher increasing dividend, from $3,51 in 2012 to $6.84 in 2025, with the following annual increases:

  • 11.11% and 7.95% in 2013 and 2014.
  • 6.97% in 2022, 6.34% in 2023, and 7.95% in 2024.

(In the other periods, the increases were quite marginal).

Chevron vs. Totalenergies

Which cash engines will support dividend returns in the coming years

Chevron and TotalEnergies have over a century of successful oil production, generating solid cash flows and providing attractive dividend returns to shareholders.

Oil will remain a significant cash generator for both companies in the coming year. However, while Chevron will focus its major capital expenditures on oil, particularly its primary project in Guyana, TotalEnergies' strategy will increasingly prioritize LNG developments.

These differing allocations of CAPEX are expected to generate different levels of cash flows and to have different impacts on dividend returns.

Chevron Oil in Guyana, along with the re-structuring of its portfolio, will support dividend returns

Chevron Corporation organizes its activity through two main business segments:

  • The upstream business unit, which primarily consists of the exploration, development, production and transportation of crude oil and natural gas (although Chevron is a major LNG producer, heavily focused on large Australian projects like Gorgon and Wheatstone for Asian markets, oil production remains the core business of this unit).
  • The downstream business unit, which primarily consists of the refining and marketing of oil and gas products.

Since 2021, Chevron has allocated about 80% of its budget to upstream investments each year. In 2023, 2024, and for the first six months of 2025, the allocation was 86.41%, 87.13%, and 92.43%, respectively.

Chevron is dedicating its CAPEX budgets to oil investments primarily in Kazakhstan, the Gulf of Mexico, the Permian Basin, and Guyana developments. The Company expects these regions to be the main contributors to its cash flow in the coming years.

In the Permian Basin, Chevron has successfully achieved its production target of 1 million barrels, and this location is now poised to become a cash flow source.

The Company will shortly redirect CAPEX resources toward its new development projects in Guyana, along with a focus on higher-return assets.

The investment in Guyana is expected to be the key investment for investors in the long-term because oil is relatively cheap to extract (below $35 per barrel), making the Country highly profitable and attractive for investments. We believe that Chevron will replicate its successful Permian development strategy by increasing activity and enhancing production efficiency, lowering the breakeven point to below $30 per barrel.

If Chevron achieves this goal, Guyana could significantly contribute to an estimated annual dividend increase of 7% from 2029 onwards. This projection considers the reshaping of its portfolio and the increasing returns on assets from other capital expenditures, along with oil prices of approximately $75-$80 per barrel, as explained below.

Oil quotations will support Chevron's investments

Oil quotations are expected to drop again in 2026 (to about $55-$60 per barrel) and to remain at low levels until the beginning of 2027, mainly due to oversupply. While demand has remained stronger than expected, global supply continues to rise; the OPEC+ cartel and U.S. production are the main drivers of this increase.

However, it is unlikely that oil prices will remain low over the next five years; oil will be too cheap to balance the market in the long-term.

In 2025 and 2026, demand is expected to rise by 800,000 to 900,000 barrels per day annually. Unless a global recession occurs, this trend is likely to continue at a similar pace in 2027 and into 2030, necessitating a significant increase in production.

Oil supply is expected to remain high in 2026 and early 2027. However, by 2028, annual supply growth is likely to fall behind yearly demand growth. When this occurs, oil prices are expected to rise, encouraging more investments in the industry.

Therefore, it is reasonable to estimate that oil quotation can be in the range of $75-$80 a barrel by 2030.

Totalenergies The LNG business and the big wave of 2027-2029

TotalEnergies' core business is the exploration and production of oil and gas.

However, in recent years, the French Company has restructured its organization to align with its long-term strategy to increase low-carbon activities and aims to become a net-zero company by 2050.

Actually, Totalenergies is organized into five business segments:

  • "Exploration & Production" includes oil and natural gas exploration and production activities (excluding LNG) in about 50 countries, as well as carbon-neutrality activities.
  • "Integrated LNG" includes the integrated gas chain, biogas, hydrogen and gas trading activities.
  • "Integrated Power" includes renewable energy activities, electricity generation, storage, trading, and B2B and B2C marketing activities.
  • "Refining & Chemicals" includes refining, base petrochemicals, polymer derivatives, biomass transformation, and trading and shipping activities.
  • "Marketing & Services" encompasses global supply and marketing activities for oil products and services, low-carbon fuels, and new energies for mobility.

In 2024, the French Company recorded 30.854 million dollars of cash flow from operating activities:

  • 56.36% from the "Exploration & Production" segment
  • 16.8% from the "Integrated LNG" segment

(Only 1% was generated by the "Integrated Power" segment).

Based on Totalenergies' strategy, oil projects will remain an important CAPEX allocation and a source of cash flow.

However, investors need to consider the increasing importance of the LNG business, whose contribution to the Company's cash flow generation was already 16.8% at the end of 2024.

Totalenergies' LNG portfolio is set to grow by 50% to reach 60 million tons in 2030, when this business unit will contribute about 40% to energy production.

The Company's sales were around 40 million tons in 2024 (30 million tons from its equity or supply portfolio and 10 million tons from the spot business).

The base of 30 million tons will grow to 45 million tons over the period.

The increase is expected to start in 2028 with ECA, the Qatari NFE, Rio Grande Trains 1 to 3, NFS in Qatar, Marsa LNG, and then, in the second part of the period, it will have Ruwais LNG in Abu Dhabi, Mozambique LNG, and Rio Grande Train 4, which Totalenergies just sanctioned.

The big wave in the LNG market

The market expects a large wave of LNG production during 2027-2030, with the risk that LNG prices could drop to low levels.

According to the International Energy Agency's report, about 300 billion cubic metres (bcm) of LNG export capacity is set to be added by 2030 (based on the official timelines of projects that have reached FID and/or are under construction). The United States and Qatar are leading the next LNG wave, together accounting for more than 70% of the incremental liquefaction capacity expected to come online by 2030.

The expected wave of LNG production will significantly influence the price in the market and the profit margin of the producing companies like Totalenergies.

In the United States, Henry Hub prices collapsed to USD 2.2/MBtu in 2024, their lowest level since 1998 (except for the 2020 pandemic period). Forward curves indicate that Henry Hub prices in the United States are expected to average USD 3.7/MBtu during 2025-2030 while European hub and Asian spot LNG prices are expected to average USD 8/MBtu and USD 8.5/MBtu in the 2025-2030 period, respectively, around 40% below the levels experienced between 2019 and 2024.

Based on current forward curves, oil-indexed LNG prices are assumed to average USD 10/MBtu between 2025 and 2030, almost 10% below their levels between 2019 and 2024.

Patrick Pouyanne (Chairman & CEO of Totalenergies) has explained how the Company is working on protecting its profit margins from the possibility of a future reduction of LNG prices. For example, Totalenergies has signed almost 8 million tons of contracts with Asian clients under long-term oil-indexed contracts and increasing its integration process.

However, previous waves in the LNG market showed that when prices are softening, buyers tend to renegotiate contracts if the selling price is significantly above the contract price. In some cases, long arbitration disputes have taken place during those periods.

The French company is making good progress in preparing for the upcoming wave; however, the strategy will be tested during a volatile period in the LNG market. For this reason, I believe TotalEnergies' cash flow projections for these projects will be lower and riskier.

Chevron vs. Totalenergies

Which is the best stock for dividends?

Final Comment

Chevron and Totalenergies are two big oil companies whose core business is the production and sale of oil and gas products. In the period 2012-2024, they generated solid cash flows from operating activities and guaranteed annual dividend increases to investors almost every year, with Chevron that has offered better returns than Totalenergies.

The two Companies have a long and successful history in oil production, which will remain the key source of cash flows. However, they have set different long-term business strategies, which will influence cash generation, and dividend returns in the coming years.

Based on the actual forecasts of oil and LNG quotations from the EIA and other international agencies, and considering the business strategies of Chevron and Totalenergies, I believe the American Company will generate stronger and low riskier cash flows for dividends than the French competitor.

I expect the two companies to offer lower increases on dividends this year and next:

  • In 2024, Chevron offered a dividend increase of 7.95%, it will be 4.91% in 2025.
  • In 2024, Totalenergies offered a dividend increase of 6.52%, it is expected to be 5.29% in 2025 (the last dividend related to 2025 has not been declared yet).

As per my forecast, Chevron will progressively increase its dividend to 7% by 2029 (this estimate is based on oil quotations at $75-$80 per barrel in 2029-2030, along with significant returns from investments in Guyana).

I do not believe that Totalenergies will offer a higher dividend increase because a significant percentage of its cash flow will depend on the new LNG projects which will start to generate cash flows during the LNG wave. In addition, Totalenergies' oil projects are not expected to perform better than Chevron's oil projects in the coming years.

Finally, I'm not expecting the two companies to reduce their dividends during a recession period. The two balance sheets are very solid, and I believe they will use leverage to maintain dividends as they did during the COVID period.

Totalenergies - Conversion of ADRs into Ordinary Shares

On September 24, 2025, Totalenergies' Board of Directors approved the project to convert ADRs into ordinary shares listed on the NYSE.

The termination of the ADR program became effective on December 8, 2025; ADRs were cancelled and ordinary shares listed on the NYSE.

All Totalenergies' ordinary shares, including those to be held or traded in the US market, are in the scope of the French Financial Transaction Tax.

For shareholders that are not French residents for tax purposes, French withholding tax is applying, regardless of whether their shares are held on the European or US market. The applicable withholding tax rate depends on the specific situation and country of tax residence of the beneficial owner.

Therefore, given that Chevron is an American company and Totalenergies is a French firm, investors must also consider the differing tax treatments when selecting the best stock for their portfolio.

data.title: Nike (NKE): Buying a Global Brand at a Cyclical Discount
data.description:
Stock Pick: Nike Inc.

Nike is a global consumer franchise trading at a valuation that reflects cyclical margin pressure, near-term operational reset, and geopolitical policy risk, but materially understates the company's enduring brand equity and long-term earnings power. At current prices, investors are paying for a temporarily impaired operating profile while receiving one of the world's most valuable consumer brands and its embedded growth optionality largely for free.

I believe Nike's intrinsic value is meaningfully higher than today's market price based on normalized margins and cash-flow generation alone. Importantly, this valuation does not require the repeal of tariffs to work. However, should tariffs be reduced or struck down, that outcome would act as a powerful incremental catalyst, accelerating margin recovery and sentiment re-rating.

Business Overview

Nike designs, markets, and distributes athletic footwear, apparel, and equipment globally. The company operates across wholesale and direct-to-consumer channels, with broad geographic exposure and category leadership in footwear. While recent years have been defined by inventory dislocations, channel recalibration, and margin compression, Nike remains a scale leader with unmatched global brand recognition.

Brand Equity as an Off-Balance-Sheet Asset

Nike's most valuable asset does not appear on its balance sheet.

The Nike brand is among the most recognized consumer brands globally, with cultural relevance extending far beyond sport. From an economic standpoint, this brand equity functions as a structural competitive advantage that lowers costs, improves capital efficiency, and creates long-dated growth optionality.

Why this matters economically:

  • Lower cost of customer acquisition relative to competitors
  • Pricing power that supports premium margins over time
  • Faster adoption curves in adjacent product categories
  • Trust and credibility that reduce friction when entering new markets

Accounting standards expense brand-building investments rather than capitalizing them. As a result, Nike's financial statements systematically undervalue its true asset base, particularly relative to asset-heavy or purely digital peers.

This off-balance-sheet brand equity is especially important as consumer products increasingly intersect with technology. Categories such as wearables, performance analytics, connected footwear, and AI-enabled training tools require consumer trust, lifestyle alignment, and global distribution all areas where Nike already has a substantial head start.

In practical terms, Nike can enter these categories with:

  • Lower marketing spend per unit of adoption
  • Built-in distribution and athlete endorsement infrastructure
  • Existing consumer data and engagement ecosystems

This optionality is real, economically meaningful, and not fully reflected in current valuation models.

Financial Profile and Normalized Earnings Power

Nike's recent financial performance reflects a business in transition rather than one in structural decline. Margin compression has been driven by:

  • Elevated inventory levels following pandemic-era demand distortions
  • Promotional activity to reset channels
  • Freight, sourcing, and input cost pressures
  • Strategic adjustments in the wholesale vs. direct mix

Despite these headwinds, Nike continues to generate substantial revenue, free cash flow, and returns on invested capital. The balance sheet remains solid, allowing the company to:

  • Invest through the cycle
  • Support innovation and brand spending
  • Return capital to shareholders

Normalization matters

When evaluating Nike, the key analytical question is not whether margins are currently under pressure they are but whether those margins are permanently impaired.

I do not believe they are.

Historically, Nike has demonstrated the ability to:

  • Operate at meaningfully higher operating margins
  • Generate strong free cash flow across cycles
  • Recover profitability following industry dislocations

A return toward normalized operating margins even without heroic assumptions supports earnings power well above what is implied by the current share price.

Valuation Framework

Nike's current valuation reflects a market pricing in:

  • Sustained margin pressure
  • Slower growth
  • Persistent geopolitical and policy risk

Using conservative assumptions:

  • Modest long-term revenue growth
  • Operating margins below historical peaks
  • No major benefit from new technology categories

Nike still appears reasonably valued to undervalued based on discounted cash flow and normalized earnings approaches.

This creates an attractive setup:

  • Downside is buffered by brand strength, cash flow, and balance sheet quality
  • Upside is driven by any combination of margin normalization, multiple re-rating, or incremental growth

In other words, investors are paying for a challenged version of Nike and receiving the franchise version for free.

Tariffs: Asymmetric Upside, Not a Required Assumption

Tariffs have weighed disproportionately on sentiment toward global consumer brands with international sourcing. Nike is no exception.

My base-case thesis does not require tariffs to be repealed. Even under current conditions, Nike's intrinsic value appears higher than today's price.

However, tariffs represent a clear asymmetric upside catalyst:

  • Legal challenges could materially weaken or overturn them
  • Political and economic realities make them difficult to sustain long term
  • Any reduction would flow directly to gross margin

Because the market is currently discounting tariffs as a semi-permanent headwind, their removal would likely:

  • Improve forward margin expectations
  • Drive earnings revisions
  • Trigger a valuation multiple re-rating

This makes tariffs icing on the cake not the cake itself.

Catalysts

Several developments could unlock value over the next 12 months:

  • Margin stabilization and recovery as inventory normalizes
  • Improved guidance as channel reset matures
  • Evidence of traction in innovation and performance technology
  • Tariff relief or legal progress, even partial
  • Sentiment re-rating as pessimism fades and fundamentals stabilize

Bear Case

The strongest bear arguments include:

  • Slower-than-expected margin recovery
  • Prolonged promotional environment
  • Intensifying competition from niche performance brands
  • Tariffs persisting longer than expected

These risks are real. However, at current valuation levels, I believe much of this downside is already priced in, while upside from normalization and optionality is not.

Conclusion

Nike today represents a rare opportunity to buy a globally dominant consumer brand at a valuation shaped by temporary dislocation rather than permanent impairment.

The company's off-balance-sheet brand equity, long-term category optionality, and normalized earnings power are not fully reflected in the current stock price. Investors are effectively underwriting a conservative base case while retaining meaningful upside from operational recovery and policy-driven catalysts.

Even without tariff relief, Nike appears undervalued relative to its intrinsic worth. If tariffs are reduced or repealed, the upside becomes meaningfully larger.

This combination of downside protection, embedded brand value, and asymmetric upside makes Nike a compelling long-term investment at current levels.

data.title: GAIL Q3 Results: Gas marketing EBIT falls, Petchem loss widens
data.description:
GAIL (India) Ltd. reported its third quarter earnings on Saturday, January 31.

Its revenue declined 27.6% to ₹1,602.6 crore from ₹2,212.2 crore in the previous year.

GAIL shares ended the previous session 0.2% lower at ₹167.05 apiece. The stock has declined nearly 3% in the past month.

data.title: Entravision
data.description:
Entravision is a US media and marketing company. I believe it is deeply undervalued because the market recognizes it only as a declining traditional media business, while overlooking its rapidly growing ad-tech companies, Smadex and Adwake. Furthermore, Entravision holds significant assets in its spectrum licenses that could conservatively be worth their entire $300 million market cap.

Entravision owns and operates a large network of Spanish-speaking TV and radio stations focusing on the Hispanic population in the USA. This segment is currently seen as unattractive and "unscreenable" because management is restructuring it at a significant cost, with no clear end date. The segment is profitable in election years due to political advertising revenue, but it typically breaks even or operates at a slight loss in non-election years like 2025. Despite this, the company historically pays a dividend, currently yielding roughly 6.2%.

Regarding their spectrum: My thesis is that under the Trump administration, there is a higher chance of new spectrum auctions, which could allow Entravision to sell some of its holdings. In 2017, the average price for spectrum was $0.93/MHz-pop. Based on this, their spectrum could have been worth $280 million in 2017 prices; adjusted for inflation, that is roughly $370 million. These are conservative estimates, as I do not intend to capture every detail influencing the final price.

Most importantly, Entravision has a rapidly growing ad-tech segment represented by Smadex and Adwake. These companies provide programmatic advertising, user acquisition, and campaign execution. To demonstrate this growth, the chart below separates the legacy media and digital advertising segments.

Last quarter, the advertising segment's revenues grew 104% YoY, and EBITDA grew 378%. I believe the market only partially recognizes the value hidden here. Although the stock price grew 40% after the last earnings report, I believe there is room for it to grow more than 100% over the next twelve months from its current price of $3.27/share.

Entravision has an enterprise value of $450 million. For my valuation, I value the legacy segment at $0 and the spectrum at $280 million. If we annualize the ad-tech segment's last quarter, we arrive at $38 million in EBITDA. A 15x multiplewhich is reasonable for this level of growthwould value the ad-tech segment at $570 million.

That is almost double the current EV. I believe 2026 will be the catalyst year for Entravision: the midterm elections will drive political spending back to the legacy segment, a potential spectrum auction could unlock asset value, and the ad-tech segment will likely continue to utilize operating leverage to deliver higher profits.

There are risks: the thesis relies heavily on ad-tech growth. If that stalls, the outlook becomes bearish. I will also be closely monitoring management's capital allocation behavior.

data.title: Real Cells, Real Sales: Why Amprius is Winning the Next-Gen Battery Race
data.description:
Clear Thesis

What they do: Amprius Technologies manufactures

high-density silicon anode lithium-ion batteries. Unlike competitors

who are still in the "science project" phase, Amprius is

commercially shipping batteries that deliver ~450 Wh/kg (vs. ~260

Wh/kg for conventional Tesla/Panasonic cells). Their primary markets

are currently aviation (drones, HAPS, eVTOL) and light electric

vehicles, where weight savings are critical.

Why it's mispriced: The market is valuing AMPX as a

speculative R&D play, but the company has quietly transitioned

into a high-growth commercial manufacturer. In Q3 2025, revenue

soared 173% YoY to $21.4M, and they are nearing EBITDA breakevena

rarity in the next-gen battery space. The recent pivot to an

"asset-light" model (using contract manufacturing partners

rather than building expensive owned factories immediately)

drastically reduces capital intensity and dilution risk, a shift the

market has not yet fully appreciated.

Why 2 is plausible: A 2x return would take the market cap

to ~$3B. Given that pre-revenue peers like QuantumScape have

historically traded at $3B$10B valuations based purely on promise,

Amprius can command a premium valuation as it proves it can

commercially scale and reach profitability. If they hit

~$100M$120M in 2026 revenue with positive EBITDA, a re-rating to a

growth-tech multiple makes $22+ highly achievable.

Valuation Anchor

Comparable Analysis & Growth Multiple:

The "Science" Premium: Competitors like QuantumScape (QS) or Solid Power (SLDP) have often traded at multi-billion dollar valuations with zero material revenue. Amprius has de-risked the technology.

Revenue Slope: AMPX is currently trading at ~15-20x forward sales (assuming ~$80M$100M next 12 months). While optically high, high-growth hardware monopolies (like Enphase in its early boom) often trade at 30x+ sales when profitable.

Intrinsic Value Step-Change: Achieving EBITDA breakeven (expected with just ~$10M more in quarterly revenue) eliminates the "bankruptcy discount" often applied to battery startups. Removing this discount alone could double the equity value as institutional capital feels safe to enter.

Catalysts

EBITDA Breakeven: Management indicated they are close to operational breakeven. Crossing this line is a massive psychological and structural catalyst for the stock, separating it from "cash incineration" peers.

Major Defense/Aviation Wins: Amprius recently secured a $35M order from a Tier 1 UAS (unmanned aircraft system) manufacturer. Announcements of follow-on orders from major defense contractors (e.g., AeroVironment, Airbus, or the U.S. Army's DIU) would validatethe technology at scale.

Partner Expansion (Asset-Light): Successful validation of their gigawatt-hour scale manufacturing partners (SiCore product line) will prove they can scale supply without spending hundreds of millions on capex.

Bear Case

Valuation & Expectations: The stock has already run up

significantly (from ~$2 lows in 2025 to ~$11 in early 2026). The

current valuation (~$1.5B) prices in a lot of perfection.

Execution Risk: If the contract manufacturing partners fail to deliver quality at volume, Amprius could miss revenue targets, causing the high multiple to compress rapidly.

Cash Burn: While breakeven is "close," if they miss it and require another massive equity raise, the resulting dilution could crush the stock price back to single digits.

Customer Concentration: A significant portion of revenue relies on a few key aviation customers. A delay in a single program could ruin a quarter.

Guru Activity

Recent institutional activity suggests "smart money" is

positioning for the next leg up:

Citadel Advisors: Increased position by ~796% (holding ~2.5M shares).

Blackrock & Vanguard: Both added significantly to their positions in late 2025, owning a combined ~14M shares.

Driehaus Capital Management: Opened a new position with ~3M shares, signaling interest from growth-focused active managers.

data.title: Why NICE Ltd May Be Mispriced
data.description:
NICE Ltd: Research Summary and Investment Outlook (as of 01/22/2026)

What NICE Does

NICE Ltd is a provider of enterprise software focused on two primary segments: customer experience and contact centers (via its CXone cloud platform enhanced by AI tools), and financial crime and compliance (through Actimize). The company primarily operates a subscription/cloud model but continues to offer services and some legacy on-premises solutions.

Market Snapshot

As of January 20, 2026, NICE's stock closed at approximately $112.08, with a market capitalization of around $7.15 billion and an enterprise value of $6.76 billion. Key valuation metrics include EV/EBITDA of about 8.28, price-to-free cash flow (P/FCF) near 9.36, trailing price-to-earnings (P/E) at 12.74, and forward P/E at 10.04. The 50-day moving average (DMA) is $112.97, while the 200-DMA stands at $142.18, indicating the stock is trading well below its longer-term trend. Short interest is moderate at 1.85 million shares (2.93% of float) and does not suggest significant bearish sentiment. The next major catalyst is the upcoming earnings report, estimated for February 19, 2026.

Why NICE May Be Mispriced

NICE is currently valued as a low-growth, legacy vendor, despite its transition toward an AI-driven, cloud-first portfolio. The market appears more focused on near-term margin pressures and competitive fears around AI rather than the company's stable cash generation. This results in an underappreciation of NICE's durable free cash flow and its potential for growth acceleration following the integration of Cognigy.

Key Drivers for a Potential Upside

  • Multiple Re-Rating: If NICE's valuation moves from ~9x P/FCF to a range of 1618xcloser to SaaS peersthe stock could nearly double, even with modest free cash flow growth.
  • Growth Re-Acceleration: The Cognigy acquisition, valued at $955 million, is expected to drive approximately 80% ARR growth in 2026, potentially shifting market perception.
  • Catalyst Proximity: The upcoming earnings report offers a near-term opportunity for NICE to reset expectations, particularly around guidance, AI annual recurring revenue (ARR), and backlog.

Valuation Anchor

NICE offers a robust free cash flow engine, with FY2024 FCF at about $733 million and trailing twelve-month FCF at $763 million. If the market normalizes to a 6% FCF yield (~16.7x P/FCF), the company's enterprise value could reach $12.7 billion, implying a market cap of nearly 1.8 times its current value. With even modest FCF growth (1015%) from AI and cloud monetization, a twofold increase in equity value is plausible if sentiment shifts.

Technical Overview

Currently, NICE trades below its 200-DMA ($142.18) and near its 50-DMA ($112.97), signaling a repair mode after a significant drawdown. The 52-week range ($99$180.61) places the stock well below its highs, with technical indicators such as RSI(14) at 44.68 reflecting stabilization but no clear upward momentum. A break above the 200-DMA would indicate a major trend reversal, while failure to hold above the 50-DMA could mean continued volatility.

Sentiment & Positioning

The company's Q3'25 results highlighted AI-first momentum, with cloud backlog up 15% year-over-year and guidance raised. Analyst opinions are generally positive, with consensus price targets between $150 and $200, though margin pressures remain a concern. Retail investors are split between those believing in deep value and those skeptical of a sustained turnaround.

Catalysts and Risks: The Next 12 Months

Top Catalysts

  • Q4/FY earnings and the 2026 outlook, focusing on AI ARR, backlog, and margins.
  • Progress on Cognigy integration, especially cross-selling and AI agent deployments.
  • Ongoing evidence of AI traction in major deals and ARR growth.
  • Share repurchases, which can amplify any re-rating if fundamentals stabilize.
  • Interest rate stability, which supports higher software valuation multiples.

Key Risks

  • Competitive threats from other AI-enabled platforms could compress pricing and retention.

data.title: Delhivery Q3 net profit surges 58% on strong volume growth, EBITDA more than doubles
data.description:
Logistics major Delhivery reported a strong set of numbers for the December quarter, with net profit rising 58.4% year-on-year to ₹39.6 crore, aided by healthy volume growth and operating leverage.

Revenue increased 18% to ₹2,804 crore compared with ₹2,378 crore in the year-ago period.

EBITDA more than doubled to ₹208.5 crore from ₹103 crore a year earlier, while operating margin improved to 7.4% from 4.3%.

The company said its Part Truck Load (PTL) business crossed 500,000 metric tonnes for the first time, registering 23% year-on-year growth, driven by consistent sales efforts and stable service quality despite a sharp rise in network volumes.

Separately, Delhivery said it has assessed the financial impact of the recently notified Labour Codes, which consolidate multiple existing labour laws into a single framework. The changes have led to an increase in gratuity and leave liabilities amounting to ₹20.86 crore, which has been classified as an exceptional item in the consolidated results for the quarter and nine months ended December 31, 2025.

The company said it continues to monitor developments and will evaluate any further impact once the final rules and effective dates are notified.

Shares of Delhivery ended higher on Friday, January 31, by 3.53% at 426.35 on the NSE.

data.title: Active Solana addresses spike 115%, four in 10 merchants take Bitcoin: Month in Charts
data.description:
Activity on major altcoin networks, namely Solana and Ethereum, saw major milestones in January. Daily active addresses on Solana consistently topped 5 million in the second half of the month.

Ethereum overtook major layer 2s in December in terms of daily active addresses after major upgrades to the network. In January, the network marked a 25% increase in daily active addresses amid efforts from developers to “future proof” Ethereum.

Seven Bitcoin (BTC) miners in the US are in a critical storm zone and may need to temporarily scale back their mining activities as a winter storm rocked power grids and left thousands without electricity.

Geopolitical concerns, namely US President Donald Trump’s supposed aspirations to acquire Greenland, have global investors wary. Bitcoin’s price fell nearly 10% from a monthly high of $97,000.

Here’s January by the numbers:

Active Solana addresses increase nearly 115% amid token launch frenzy

The Solana network saw a monthly spike of 115% in active daily addresses as of Jan. 28. The total number of such addresses regularly topped 5 million, according to data from Nansen.

The surge is the result of a renewed spree in memecoin minting following the launch of Anthropic’s Claude Cowork, an AI agent that can control a user’s desktop. This allowed developers using Solana-based token launchpad Bags to turn token launches into overdrive.

Fees on the platform spiked to $4.5 million on Jan. 16. For context, from September to December last year, daily fees rarely passed five digits and, sometimes, were as low as several hundred dollars.

Over the same period, the number of tokens that “graduated,” or launched, from Bags overtook the other popular Solana token launch platform Pump.fun.

Active Ethereum addresses increase 25%

Activity on the Ethereum network has also seen a significant uptick. At the end of December, it overtook prominent L2s Base and Arbitrum in terms of daily active addresses. In January, the same metric increased 25%.

The increase in activity follows some important upgrades to the network, which have increased blob sizes and therefore lowered fees. On Jan. 29, average fees on Ethereum were less than $0.01.

These upgrades were part of an effort to finalize work on Ethereum. On Jan. 12, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin said that Ethereum should ultimately pass a “walkaway test.” He said the true test of Ethereum would be for it to keep functioning and fulfilling the needs of users without the presence of developers actively changing and monitoring the network.

Seven US Bitcoin miners face curtailment during winter storm

Seven Bitcoin mining operations in the United States may curtail operations as winter storms put stress on the American power grid in the Southeast and South Central regions.

According to data from Matthew Sigel, head of digital assets research at VanEck, mining locations operated by Riot, Core Scientific, CleanSpark and Bitdeer “are structurally set up to act as flexible loads via utility demand response programs.” 

“We do not yet have confirmation of real time curtailments for this storm, but the model has already proven its value when conditions tighten.”

The storm, which has also affected the Midwest and Northeast, has seen cancelled flights, dangerous travel conditions and power outages and has killed at least 20 people as of Jan. 27.

Southern states, which are generally unaccustomed to snow and lack the critical infrastructure to contend with wintry conditions, were hit hardest. As of Jan. 28, some 400,000 people were without power in Kentucky, Tennessee, Mississippi, Louisiana and Texas.

Many Bitcoin miners have set up in locations where they can stabilize grid prices, buying power cheaply when there is little to no demand and temporarily switching off during stress periods.

Four in 10 merchants in US accept crypto: PayPal report

Crypto is getting more popular for payments, according to major payments processor PayPal. Four in 10 merchants in the US now accept crypto, the company said in a January report. PayPal’s survey found that crypto offers faster transaction speeds and more privacy and attracts crypto-savvy customers.

PayPal vice president and general manager May Zabaneh said, “What we’re seeing both in this data and in conversations with our customers is that crypto payments are moving beyond experimentation and into everyday commerce.”

Some 84% of the same merchants believe that crypto payments will become mainstream in the next five years.

Bitcoin’s price static amid Greenland fiasco

Bitcoin’s price saw a brief climb toward $100,000 in the middle of this month before falling back down to $87,000. The more than 10% decrease came amid discussions over what could happen to Greenland, itself an autonomous territory of Denmark.

Trump claimed that the US needs to control Greenland for security purposes and to counteract Chinese and Russian ambitions in the Arctic. This is despite the fact Denmark and the US are part of NATO, an organization created to counteract the very same ambitions.

While tempers have cooled, the fact that Bitcoin, along with global markets generally, was affected by the saber-rattling, shows that BTC is a risk-on asset.

Chris Beauchamp, chief market analyst at investing and trading platform IG, said, “Cryptocurrencies offered no haven from the wave of selling that washed over global markets in response to Trump’s threat.”

Trump’s mercurial foreign policy, including punitive, unilateral tariffs and ramping up aggressive rhetoric with former allies, put a damper on Bitcoin’s price, according to some analysts.

data.title: 'Bearish engulfing' patterns are warning you: Don't buy the dip in gold-miner stocks
data.description:
By Tomi Kilgore

A reversal pattern in a gold miner ETF, and in the stock of sector heavyweight Newmont, warns investors not to buy the dip

A bearish technical pattern that appeared in a widely traded gold-mining ETF is warning investors to think twice before buying the dip.

The sharp selloff in gold-miner stocks on Friday may leave investors asking themselves whether they should use it as an opportunity to buy on the cheap. Here's why the charts say no.

While gold's (GC00) rally to record prices topping $5,500 an ounce grabbed the headlines, the gold-miner sector outperformed the metal by a wide margin. Over the past 12 months, the VanEck Gold Miners ETF GDX has soared 139%, while the SPDR Gold Shares exchange-traded fund GLD climbed 72.4%.

The idea was that at current prices, gold-miner profits would increase faster than gold prices.

But on Friday, GLD dropped 10.3%, while GDX tumbled 12.8% - its biggest one-day selloff since the height of the pandemic panic in March 2020 - with all of its U.S.-listed stock components trading lower.

Trading volume for GDX, which many technicians see as a sign of validation, swelled to more than 100 million shares on Friday, more than four times the daily average.

The fundamental reason given for the selloff appeared to be that President Trump's pick of Kevin Warsh to be the next chair of the Federal Reserve prompted a big bounce in the U.S. dollar DXY, which is viewed as bearish for commodities such as gold and silver (SI00).

But Friday's GDX selloff isn't what should make buyers think twice, or worry those who already own gold-miner stocks. The real technical trouble started a day earlier for the GDX ETF, as well as for a number of its components - the biggest being Newmont (NEM).

On Wednesday, GDX opened at $110.38 and closed at a record high of $112.16. Then on Thursday, the stock opened further in record territory at $113.29, but quickly reversed course to end the day at $107.98, or well below Wednesday's open.

For those who use candlestick charts, which were developed centuries ago in Japan, that pattern is referred to as a "bearish engulfing." The idea of the pattern is that Thursday's open marked a buying climax, allowing bears to launch a successful counterstrike.

Followers of Western charting styles will often refer to the same pattern as a "key reversal day."

Whatever you call it, it warns of a reversal in trend.

So the sharp selloff on Friday is actually viewed as confirmation of the bearish call; if bulls had much fight left in them, the GDX ETF wouldn't have fallen so much.

And it's not just the GDX chart in which the pattern appeared.

The stock chart of Newmont - GDX's largest component with a market capitalization of $124.5 billion - also saw a bearish engulfing appear on Thursday. So did the chart of the fund's second-biggest component, Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM), with a market cap of $96.7 billion.

In all, nine of the GDX ETF's 22 U.S.-listed components saw similar bearish engulfing patterns form on Thursday, after record closes on Wednesday. Two others saw the pattern appear on Monday.

As with any technical pattern, the calls they make are never perfect. But at the very least, investors should think twice about buying the dip, and wait for another sign that suggests bulls have regained their strength.

Don't miss: 7 key candlestick reversal patterns.

Some downside price levels for GDX to keep an eye on for potential support signals start at just below $90, where the 50-day moving average ended on Friday. That's a widely followed short-term trend tracker that has helped halt pullbacks in late October and early November, as well as in July.

Below that, the $84 to $85 level could provide support, as that marked a battleground between bulls and bears - acting as resistance on the way up and support on pullbacks - from mid-October through the end of December, just before GDX took off in early January.

-Tomi Kilgore

data.title: JOHNNIE WALKER TOASTS MUSIC'S BIGGEST WEEKEND WITH THE GO GO HIGHBALL
data.description:
Johnnie Walker/ Key word(s): Product Launch

JOHNNIE WALKER TOASTS MUSIC'S BIGGEST WEEKEND WITH THE GO GO HIGHBALL

31.01.2026 / 12:25 CET/CEST

The issuer is solely responsible for the content of this announcement.

A star-studded weekend of events puts the Johnnie Walker Black Label 'Go Go Highball' center stage.

KEY HIGHLIGHTS

  • Johnnie Walker, the world's number one Scotch Whisky brand (IWSR 2024), celebrates in Los Angeles with activations inspired by partner Sabrina Carpenter across music's biggest weekend.
  • Sabrina Carpenter's signature Johnnie Walker Black Label cocktail – The Go Go Highball – becomes the cocktail of the moment to toast to this year's achievements in music.
  • Johnnie Walker serves as exclusive spirits partner at major weekend events, with surround-sound presence at the weekends' biggest parties.
  • For a limited time, the Johnnie Walker Black Label Go Go Highball will be available at select Los Angeles bars including Melrose Umbrella, Roger Room and Hotel Figueroa

LOS ANGELES, Jan. 31, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- This weekend, Johnnie Walker, the world's number one Scotch Whisky brand (IWSR 2024), is celebrating music's biggest night in Los Angeles inspired by global partner, GRAMMY Award-winning artist and international superstar Sabrina Carpenter – with her signature serve – the Go Go Highball – set to become the drink of the moment.

As part of the next phase of this iconic partnership, originally announced in summer 2025, Johnnie Walker is celebrating with partner Sabrina Carpenter, who is nominated for 6 GRAMMY awards, with a weekend of high-impact moments across the city – from a striking cocktail projection to head-turning pre-and-post party experiences with Universal Music Group official weekend events and more. Landing squarely at the intersection of music, culture and celebration, Johnnie Walker is showing up at the biggest moment in the music calendar, continuing to partner with cultural trailblazers, and injecting new energy into the whisky category.

The Go Go Highball – Sabrina Carpenter's signature Johnnie Walker Black Label serve inspired by a track on her latest album – was the centerpiece of Johnnie Walker celebrations during music's biggest weekend. Kicking off the awards' weekend in style, Johnnie Walker lit up the skyline of Los Angeles with large scale animations, and hosted a Sabrina Carpenter-inspired exclusive party at the legendary Max and Helen's on Larchmont Boulevard. The Los Angeles hotspot was transformed into a fantasyland with cherry-themed décor and floral accents blending pop romance, playful glamour and after-hours indulgence.

Throughout the weekend, Johnnie Walker is taking center stage as the exclusive spirits partner at some of the city's biggest, most star-studded pre-and-post celebration events – making the Go Go Highball the must-have drink for the industry's leading music labels, nominees and winners.

In a whimsical nod to Sabrina's sparkling style and the weekend's red carpet glamour, Johnnie Walker has also created a bespoke crystal embellished Highball clutch bag as a memento of the weekend for friends of the brand.

For a limited time across Los Angeles, and to celebrate this big weekend, the Johnnie Walker Black Label Go Go Highball will also be available for consumers to try at select bars including Melrose Umbrella, Roger Room and Hotel Figueroa.

Evolving the Johnnie Walker x Sabrina Carpenter Partnership

"This moment is the perfect celebration of our partnership with Sabrina: raising a Go Go Highball to her progress and the incredible milestones she has achieved as an artist, against the backdrop of music's biggest weekend" said John Williams, Global Head of Whiskey at Diageo. "Working hand-in-hand with the newest voice in the Johnnie Walker story, we're excited to continue bringing a vibrant energy to the whisky experience."

This weekend of collaborative celebration marks a significant and exciting evolution in the partnership between Johnnie Walker and Sabrina Carpenter, which originally launched ahead of the Short n' Sweet tour in August 2025.

By partnering with voices like Sabrina, the brand continues to tell unique stories of progress and reshape the future of how and where people enjoy Johnnie Walker Black Label, from iconic stadiums and music venues to nightlife destinations across Los Angeles and beyond.
